claimText: Transgenic crops are a subset of genetically modified organisms that specifically contain DNA sequences from another species.
evidenceExcerpt: Transgenics are a subset containing DNA from another species (e.g., Bt Cotton).
examUse: Statement-based question distinguishing between general GMOs and transgenics.
distractorHooks: Transgenics contain altered DNA from the same species

claimText: Bt cotton is the only genetically modified crop currently approved for commercial cultivation in India.
evidenceExcerpt: In India, Bt cotton is the only approved GM crop for cultivation.
examUse: Fact-based statement in a multi-statement question about the status of GM crops in India.
distractorHooks: Bt brinjal is approved for cultivation
Multiple GM crops are approved for cultivation in India

claimText: Bt-brinjal is under a moratorium in India despite having received clearance from the GEAC.
evidenceExcerpt: Bt-brinjal is under a moratorium despite GEAC clearance.
examUse: Statement-based question on the regulatory status and history of specific GM crops.
distractorHooks: Bt-brinjal is approved for commercial cultivation
GEAC rejected the clearance for Bt-brinjal

claimText: The conditional approval of GM mustard (DMH-11) is currently under review by the Supreme Court of India.
evidenceExcerpt: GM mustard (DMH-11) conditional approval is under Supreme Court review.
examUse: Current affairs statement in a question about the regulatory status of GM Mustard.
distractorHooks: GM mustard has unconditional approval for cultivation
GM mustard was rejected by the GEAC without Supreme Court involvement
